target.name: Asimov

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $800M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Asimov, a Series B biotech company, commands a current post-money valuation of $800M following $209M in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage companies in the Biotech & Health sector, with 4 direct stage-peer comparables available: Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x typical step-up multiplier from Series B to subsequent rounds, implying a valuation of $1.5B. Peer-median analysis across the 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.3B, with the comp range spanning $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via the Biotech & Health median P/S of 7.4x cannot be applied, as Asimov's revenue remains undisclosed.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $1.1B (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $1.4B. This represents a modest step-up from the current $800M post-money. Key uncertainties include revenue visibility and relative positioning within the wide peer dispersion ($600M–$3.3B), which reflects substantial heterogeneity in Series B biotech valuations.
